title: Module exports a public function with no Pester smoke test
description: A .psm1 module declares a function in Export-ModuleMember (or implicitly exports via no-block module) but no companion <ModuleName>.Tests.ps1 in tests/ contains a Describe/It that invokes that function. Untested public surface drifts: signatures change, parameter names get refactored (see P042), and consumers crash at runtime in ways that would have been caught by a 30-second smoke. This pattern enforces a minimum-viable test obligation per exported function.
detection_method: For each modules/*.psm1 collect Export-ModuleMember -Function names. For each name, search tests/<ModuleName>.Tests.ps1 (and tests/SIN-*.Tests.ps1) for at least one Describe/Context/It block that references the function name. Functions with zero references are flagged.
preventionRule: Whenever a module adds or renames an exported function, add or update a corresponding It block in tests/<ModuleName>.Tests.ps1 that calls the function with valid parameters and asserts at least one observable outcome. CI should fail when an exported function has no test coverage.
remedy: Author a tests/<ModuleName>.Tests.ps1 with BeforeAll{Import-Module ...} and at least one It per exported function. Mock destructive cmdlets (Stop-Process, Remove-Item, Invoke-RestMethod). Wire into Run-AllTests.ps1.

first_instance.fix_summary: Added 7 Pester tests covering all 5 exported functions; mocks Stop-Process/Stop-Service; KILLSWITCH_NO_SELFTERMINATE env guard prevents test-runner suicide.

examples_bad: # modules/Foo.psm1 ends with: Export-ModuleMember -Function Bar  -- and tests/Foo.Tests.ps1 does not exist
examples_good: Export-ModuleMember -Function Bar  +  tests/Foo.Tests.ps1: Describe 'Foo' { It 'Bar returns expected' { Bar -X 1 | Should -Be 2 } }
